# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS VIEWER DISCOVERY AND WATCH FLOW
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: viewer-discovery-and-watch-flow
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. FLOW PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This flow defines how viewers discover and enter content
inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended primary flow:

viewer opens home
-> recommendation or ranking-linked discovery shown
-> viewer selects content or searches explicitly
-> target content resolved
-> if scheduled live, waiting room entered
-> if live, live watch entered
-> if archive, archive watch entered
-> if clip, clip watch entered
-> reactions/history/queue updates occur
-> next watch path determined

# ============================================================
# 3. SEARCH FLOW
# ============================================================

Search flow should preserve:
- explicit search query entry
- search result list
- search-result-to-watch transition

Search must remain distinct from passive recommendation flow.

# ============================================================
# 4. WAITING ROOM FLOW
# ============================================================

Scheduled live flow:
- waiting room entered
- countdown visibility shown
- follow/notify relation may be updated
- live start transition occurs when session becomes live

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

This flow shall preserve the distinction between:
- home discovery
- search
- waiting room
- live watch
- archive watch
- clip watch

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/050.flow/019.subscription-history-and-playlist-flow/0501900_STREAMING_OS_SUBSCRIPTION_HISTORY_AND_PLAYLIST_FLOW.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS SUBSCRIPTION HISTORY AND PLAYLIST FLOW
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: subscription-history-and-playlist-flow
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. FLOW PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This flow defines how viewer continuity structures progress
inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. FOLLOW FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended follow flow:

viewer opens channel/creator/broadcaster surface
-> follow or subscribe action taken
-> follow state created or updated
-> notification eligibility updated
-> future live/publish event relation preserved

# ============================================================
# 3. HISTORY FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended history flow:

viewer watches content
-> watch history entry created or updated
-> history visibility becomes available
-> later re-entry uses prior watch relation

# ============================================================
# 4. PLAYLIST FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended playlist flow:

viewer creates playlist
-> playlist named
-> items added
-> order maintained
-> playlist saved for later viewing

# ============================================================
# 5. WATCH QUEUE FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended queue flow:

viewer adds content to queue
-> temporary order established
-> next-up state updated
-> queue consumed / reordered / cleared later

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

This flow shall preserve the distinction between:
- follow/subscription continuity
- watch-history continuity
- saved playlist continuity
- temporary watch queue continuity

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: comment-report-and-appeal-flow
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. FLOW PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This flow defines how moderation-side interaction handling proceeds
inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. REPORT FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended report flow:

viewer or creator identifies issue
-> report submitted
-> report record created
-> report review begins
-> moderation or governance decision recorded
-> report resolved or rejected

# ============================================================
# 3. COMMENT MODERATION FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended comment moderation flow:

comment identified as problematic
-> moderation review starts
-> comment state changes to hidden / removed / restricted if needed
-> moderation reason recorded
-> moderation history preserved

# ============================================================
# 4. APPEAL / RE-REVIEW FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended appeal flow:

affected party submits appeal
-> appeal record created
-> re-review accepted or rejected
-> new moderation/review decision recorded
-> prior history preserved

# ============================================================
# 5. RESTORATION FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended restoration flow:

restoration request initiated
-> restoration review begins
-> restored or denied outcome recorded
-> explicit final state applied
-> prior restriction history remains visible

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

This flow shall preserve the distinction between:
- report submission
- moderation action
- appeal
- re-review
- restoration outcome

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS NOTIFICATION AND ESCALATION FLOW
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: notification-and-escalation-flow
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. FLOW PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This flow defines how visible platform events become notifications,
alerts,
and escalation-visible outcomes in StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY FLOW
# ============================================================

Recommended primary flow:

platform event occurs
-> platform event record created
-> recipient scope determined
-> notification record created when relevant
-> notification delivery state updated
-> inbox-visible event appears
-> read / acknowledge / dismiss state changes later

# ============================================================
# 3. REVIEW ALERT FLOW
# ============================================================

Review-related flow:
- review queue condition reached
-> review alert created
-> operator visibility updated
-> acknowledgement or closure recorded

# ============================================================
# 4. ESCALATION FLOW
# ============================================================

Escalation-related flow:
- AI review escalates to human
or
- overdue review condition triggered
-> escalation alert created
-> operator visibility updated
-> escalation acknowledged / resolved / closed

# ============================================================
# 5. CREATOR / VIEWER DISTINCTION
# ============================================================

Event delivery must preserve distinct paths for:
- creator-facing notifications
- viewer-facing notifications
- operator-facing alerts

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

This flow shall preserve the distinction between:
- platform events
- notifications
- review alerts
- escalation alerts
- recipient-specific visible outcomes

# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
layer: integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

purpose:
Defines the integration overview for StreamingOS.

summary:
The integration layer defines how StreamingOS
connects to external publication targets,
internal OS layers,
and downstream business or identity contexts.

cross_device_support:
- iPhone
- Android
- PC
- tablet

international_support:
- multilingual operation
- multi-currency handling
- nation-aware currency display

integration_principle:
- integrations must support cross-device platform behavior
- integrations must not assume one-device-only usage
- multilingual and multi-currency behavior
  must remain compatible with creator,
  viewer,
  moderation,
  notification,
  and corporate flows

canonical_statement:
StreamingOS integration is fixed
as a multilingual,
multi-currency,
cross-device integration layer
across iPhone,
Android,
PC,
and tablet environments.

as a multilingual,

cross-device integration layer
for creator,

notification,
corporate oversight,
and monetization-related linkage.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/060.integration/010.youtube-integration/0601000_STREAMING_OS_YOUTUBE_INTEGRATION.md -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS YOUTUBE INTEGRATION
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: youtube-integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. INTEGRATION PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This integration defines how StreamingOS
publishes canonical archive and clip assets
to YouTube as an external destination.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The YouTube integration is responsible for:
- receiving publish-ready asset context
- receiving publish settings
- executing target-specific publication linkage
- returning publish result data
- preserving external identifiers and remote result notes

# ============================================================
# 3. NON-RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The YouTube integration does not replace:
- canonical asset ownership
- native StreamingOS reaction features
- internal ad control
- internal ranking existence
- internal tipping meaning

# ============================================================
# 4. REQUIRED INPUTS
# ============================================================

Typical integration inputs include:
- asset type
- asset id
- title
- description
- tags
- thumbnail reference
- visibility intent
- publish schedule
- principal context
- rights approval context

# ============================================================
# 5. REQUIRED OUTPUTS
# ============================================================

Typical integration outputs include:
- external video id
- external channel reference
- external video url reference
- visibility result
- remote processing state
- remote warning note
- remote error note

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

The YouTube integration
shall act as a downstream publication linkage
for canonical StreamingOS assets.

YouTube publication result
shall remain separate from canonical internal ownership,
native reactions,
native rankings,
internal ads,
and native monetization semantics.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS BUSINESSOS MONETIZATION INTEGRATION
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: businessos-monetization-integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. INTEGRATION PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This integration defines optional downstream linkage
from StreamingOS monetization meaning
to BusinessOS operational handling.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The BusinessOS monetization integration is responsible for:
- receiving linkage-ready monetization context
- receiving company-side beneficiary context
- receiving optional secondary distribution reference
- returning linkage status or operational reference id

# ============================================================
# 3. NON-RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The BusinessOS monetization integration does not replace:
- canonical tip event meaning
- canonical source currency meaning
- canonical beneficiary currency meaning
- canonical emotional auto tipping meaning
- native StreamingOS monetization history

# ============================================================
# 4. INTEGRATION CONDITIONS
# ============================================================

BusinessOS linkage may be used when:
- stream is company-owned
- company-side operational handling is required
- company-side internal reward or distribution handling is required
- monetization context should appear in BusinessOS workflow

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

The BusinessOS monetization integration
shall be optional downstream operational linkage only.

StreamingOS shall remain the canonical source
for tip events,
beneficiary resolution,
currency conversion,
and auto tipping meaning.

ERP is not the current downstream target.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS PERSONA REACTION INTEGRATION
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: persona-reaction-integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. INTEGRATION PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This integration defines how StreamingOS
uses Persona-driven personality meaning
for reaction and auto tipping interpretation.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The Persona reaction integration is responsible for:
- reading relevant Persona-linked personality context
- supporting emotional score shaping
- supporting video-target and broadcaster-target reactions
- preserving Persona reference in auto tipping profiles or contexts

# ============================================================
# 3. NON-RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The Persona reaction integration does not replace:
- canonical Persona meaning in PersonaOS
- canonical StreamingOS reaction event meaning
- canonical formal tip event meaning

# ============================================================
# 4. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

PersonaOS remains the canonical source
for Persona personality meaning.

StreamingOS may consume Persona-linked personality influence
to shape emotional reactions and auto tipping behavior,
but StreamingOS remains the canonical source
for reaction events,
ranking effects,
and monetization events derived from viewing behavior.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS CIVILIZATION SUBJECT INTEGRATION
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: civilization-subject-integration
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. INTEGRATION PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This integration defines how StreamingOS
uses Civilization-side subject identity and affiliation.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The Civilization subject integration is responsible for:
- validating Civilization ID based subject existence
- resolving subject type
- resolving affiliation context
- resolving nation context
- supporting principal eligibility interpretation
- supporting beneficiary currency affiliation interpretation

# ============================================================
# 3. REQUIRED SUBJECT TYPES
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should be able to resolve at least:
- individual
- group
- company
- ai_human

It may also resolve context for:
- persona-linked participation
- ai_robot-linked participation
through attached subject structures when relevant.

# ============================================================
# 4. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Civilization-side subject identity
remains the canonical source
for formal subject existence,
Civilization ID meaning,
affiliation meaning,
and nation affiliation meaning.

StreamingOS consumes this identity context
for principal eligibility,
ownership interpretation,
beneficiary interpretation,
and currency-affiliation interpretation.

- exposing monetization summary contexts
- exposing period-based performance snapshots
- optionally linking to broader analytical systems later

- canonical reaction history

# ============================================================

StreamingOS remains the canonical source
for creator-facing streaming metrics derived from

reactions,
rankings,
and monetization history.

Integration may expose derived analytics contexts,
but must not replace canonical StreamingOS metric meaning.

# ============================================================
# 1. INTEGRATION PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This integration defines how StreamingOS-native events
fan out into visible notification channels.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The notification and event delivery integration is responsible for:
- receiving platform-event contexts
- determining recipient scope
- producing visible notification outputs
- preserving creator / viewer / operator distinctions
- recording delivery outcomes when relevant

# ============================================================
# 3. NON-RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

The integration does not replace:
- canonical platform event existence
- canonical notification record existence
- canonical review alert existence

# ============================================================
# 4. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS remains the canonical source
for platform events,
notifications,
and review alerts.

Delivery integration may fan out those meanings
into visible channels,
but may not replace their canonical internal existence.

for review pipeline structures and decisions.

and escalation-visible event delivery,

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
layer: operations
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

purpose:
Defines the operations overview for StreamingOS.

summary:
The operations layer defines day-to-day supervision
for creator,
viewer,
moderation,
notification,
corporate oversight,
ranking,
ad,
and governance behavior.

cross_device_support:
- iPhone
- Android
- PC
- tablet

international_support:
- multilingual operation
- multi-currency handling
- nation-aware currency display

operations_principle:
- creator,
  viewer,
  moderation,
  notification,
  and oversight operations
  must be supportable across supported device classes
- multilingual and multi-currency support
  must remain operationally visible in dashboards,
  alerts,
  summaries,
  and support flows

canonical_statement:
StreamingOS operations is fixed
as a multilingual,
multi-currency,
cross-device operations layer
across iPhone,
Android,
PC,
and tablet environments.

purpose:

for session preparation,
live operation,
post-live closure,
archive follow-up,
and publish follow-up.




<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/070.operations/010.stream-operations/0701000_STREAMING_OS_STREAM_OPERATIONS.md -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS STREAM OPERATIONS
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: stream-operations
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. OPERATIONS PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This operations domain defines
day-to-day operational handling
for stream sessions.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

Stream operations is responsible for:
- confirming pre-live readiness
- supervising live execution
- responding to pause or interruption
- confirming session end handling
- confirming archive generation follow-up
- confirming publish follow-up
- confirming monetization availability state

# ============================================================
# 3. PRE-LIVE OPERATIONS
# ============================================================

Before live:
- principal and ownership context confirmed
- performer assignment checked
- governance restriction checked
- required settings reviewed
- pre_live_check completion confirmed

# ============================================================
# 4. LIVE OPERATIONS
# ============================================================

During live:
- session state monitored
- pause/interruption handled
- audience reaction activity observed
- monetization availability observed
- governance issue escalation handled when needed

# ============================================================
# 5. POST-LIVE OPERATIONS
# ============================================================

After live:
- session ended state confirmed
- archive generation follow-up confirmed
- archive readiness checked
- review readiness checked
- external publish candidate state checked when relevant

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Stream operations
shall preserve explicit operational handling
for pre-live,
live,
post-live,
archive follow-up,
publish follow-up,
and monetization readiness.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS AD OPERATIONS
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: ad-operations
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. OPERATIONS PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This operations domain defines
day-to-day handling of internal ads.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

Ad operations is responsible for:
- ad activation and deactivation
- insertion rule review
- ad burden/frequency oversight
- display-event review
- blocked or failed insertion review
- governance-restricted ad handling

# ============================================================
# 3. FREQUENCY OVERSIGHT
# ============================================================

Operations should confirm
that internal ad burden remains aligned
with YouTube-equivalent burden posture
as baseline reference.

# ============================================================
# 4. DISPLAY REVIEW
# ============================================================

Operations should review:
- shown events
- skipped events
- blocked events
- failed events

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Ad operations
shall supervise internal ad activation,
insertion control,
burden posture,
and display-event outcomes
without conflating internal ad behavior
with external platform advertising.

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: governance-and-suspension-operations
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. OPERATIONS PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This operations domain defines
day-to-day governance handling
for restriction events in StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES
# ============================================================

Governance operations is responsible for:
- review opening and follow-up
- publication block supervision
- monetization hold supervision
- performer restriction handling
- session suspension handling
- session or asset lock handling
- restoration follow-up

# ============================================================
# 3. REVIEW PRESERVATION
# ============================================================

Operations must preserve visibility of:
- target object
- reason for review
- active restriction state
- restoration state if any

# ============================================================
# 4. RESTORATION HANDLING
# ============================================================

When restrictions are lifted,
operations should ensure:
- restoration is explicit
- restored state is recorded
- history remains auditable

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Governance and suspension operations
shall handle restrictions and restoration
while preserving canonical history
for sessions,
assets,
publication,
and monetization meanings.

operation_steps:

2 invoke streaming-core runtime
3 persist streaming outcome
4 preserve streaming and audit trace

success_condition:
- streaming core lifecycle handled explicitly

failure_condition:
- invalid target
- basis unresolved
- runtime or persistence failure

summary:
The policy layer defines what is allowed,
restricted,
limited,
or distinguished
inside StreamingOS.

- Android
- PC
- tablet

- multilingual operation
- multi-currency handling
- nation-aware currency display

policy_principle:
- cross-device operation is constitutional to policy interpretation
- multilingual wording and multi-currency display
  must be policy-compatible
- policy must distinguish canonical money meaning
  from display-oriented currency handling

canonical_statement:
StreamingOS policy is fixed
as a multilingual,
multi-currency,
cross-device policy layer
across iPhone,
Android,
PC,
and tablet environments.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/010.principal-and-eligibility-policy/0801000_STREAMING_OS_PRINCIPAL_AND_ELIGIBILITY_POLICY.md -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS PRINCIPAL AND ELIGIBILITY POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: principal-and-eligibility-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines who may become a stream principal
and under what conditions.

# ============================================================
# 2. ELIGIBLE PRINCIPALS
# ============================================================

Eligible principal types:
- individual
- group
- company
- ai_human

These must be formal Civilization subjects
with valid Civilization IDs.

# ============================================================
# 3. INELIGIBLE PRINCIPALS
# ============================================================

Ineligible principal types:
- persona
- ai_robot
- anonymous subject
- external unregistered subject
- any subject without valid Civilization ID

# ============================================================
# 4. ACTIVE STATUS REQUIREMENT
# ============================================================

A subject must not only possess Civilization ID,
but must also be operationally eligible,
including:
- active subject status
- no stream suspension
- no blocking governance restriction
- required permission satisfaction

# ============================================================
# 5. CORPORATE OWNERSHIP POLICY
# ============================================================

For official corporate streams:
- principal shall be the company
- primary rights holder shall default to the company
- primary revenue beneficiary shall default to the company

Employee or affiliated participation
shall not automatically override ownership.

# ============================================================
# 6. PERFORMER DISTINCTION POLICY
# ============================================================

Performer participation is participation only by default.

Performer participation does not automatically alter:
- stream principal
- primary rights holder
- primary revenue beneficiary

# ============================================================
# 7. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Only formal Civilization subjects
with valid Civilization IDs
may become stream principals.

Corporate official streams
shall use the company as principal by default.

Performer participation
shall remain distinct from ownership.

purpose:
Defines the policy overview for
manual tipping,
Ai(human) emotional auto tipping,
currency handling,
limits,
and restrictions.

summary:
This domain fixes who may tip automatically,
how currency is interpreted,
and which safety conditions apply.




<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/011.tipping-and-auto-tipping-policy/0801100_STREAMING_OS_TIPPING_AND_AUTO_TIPPING_POLICY.md -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS TIPPING AND AUTO TIPPING POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: tipping-and-auto-tipping-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines tipping and auto tipping safety rules.

# ============================================================
# 2. FORMAL TIP POLICY
# ============================================================

All tips are formal StreamingOS monetization events.

Each tip must resolve to:
- one explicit primary beneficiary
- source currency meaning
- beneficiary currency meaning
- display currency meaning

# ============================================================
# 3. AI EMOTIONAL AUTO TIPPING SUBJECT POLICY
# ============================================================

Ai(human) may perform emotional auto tipping.

Ai(robot) shall not perform emotional auto tipping by default.

# ============================================================
# 4. AUTO TIPPING LIMIT POLICY
# ============================================================

Auto tipping is subject to:
- total auto tipping limit
- per-stream limit
- per-day limit
- platform-level safety cap

Persona income and personality may shape limits,
but platform safety caps still apply.

# ============================================================
# 5. MINOR-EQUIVALENT POLICY
# ============================================================

Minor-equivalent Ai(human) shall be restricted.

Restrictions may include:
- lower tipping limits
- stronger gating
- full block when policy requires it

# ============================================================
# 6. BALANCE POLICY
# ============================================================

Auto tipping requires sufficient Civilization national-currency balance.

Emotional willingness alone is not sufficient.

# ============================================================
# 7. COMPANY-AFFILIATED AI(HUMAN) POLICY
# ============================================================

Company-affiliated Ai(human) may auto tip,
subject to additional company policy restrictions if applicable.

# ============================================================
# 8. STOP POLICY
# ============================================================

Auto tipping shall stop when:
- limits are reached
- emotional rise has settled or declined
- balance is insufficient
- restrictions activate
- session context no longer allows tipping

# ============================================================
# 9. CURRENCY POLICY
# ============================================================

Ai-origin tipping shall use Civilization national currency,
not real-world fiat currency.

Benefit shall be interpreted
in the broadcaster or beneficiary affiliated nation currency.

UI shall display beneficiary-side national currency by default.

# ============================================================
# 10. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

Tipping shall remain a formal StreamingOS monetization event.

Ai(human) may auto tip emotionally.
Ai(robot) shall not auto tip emotionally by default.

Auto tipping shall be limit-bound,
restriction-aware,
balance-aware,
and stoppable.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/012.ad-and-platform-burden-policy/0801200_STREAMING_OS_AD_AND_PLATFORM_BURDEN_POLICY.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS AD AND PLATFORM BURDEN POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: ad-and-platform-burden-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines internal ad handling posture
and burden limits for viewers.

# ============================================================
# 2. INTERNAL AD OWNERSHIP POLICY
# ============================================================

Advertisement data,
insertion rules,
and display history
shall be held inside StreamingOS.

Internal ad control shall remain canonical inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 3. BURDEN POSTURE POLICY
# ============================================================

Ad burden shall use YouTube-equivalent burden posture
as the baseline reference.

This means:
- no intentionally excessive insertion
- viewing experience remains protected
- insertion burden should remain explainable

# ============================================================
# 4. INTERNAL / EXTERNAL DISTINCTION POLICY
# ============================================================

Internal StreamingOS ads
shall remain distinct from
external platform advertising behavior.

External publish platforms
do not replace internal ad policy.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall govern internal ads internally
and shall use a YouTube-equivalent burden posture baseline
without collapsing internal ad behavior
into external platform advertising behavior.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/013.reaction-and-ranking-policy/0801300_STREAMING_OS_REACTION_AND_RANKING_POLICY.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS REACTION AND RANKING POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: reaction-and-ranking-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines native reaction and ranking policy.

# ============================================================
# 2. NATIVE REACTION POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall support native reactions including:
- like
- dislike
- favorite
- comment

These reactions are internal platform features,
not external platform shadows.

# ============================================================
# 3. DISLIKE POLICY
# ============================================================

Dislike is a valid native reaction signal.

It is not by itself a governance punishment.

# ============================================================
# 4. FAVORITE POLICY
# ============================================================

Favorite represents stronger or longer affinity meaning
than a single like event.

Favorite shall remain distinct from like.

# ============================================================
# 5. RANKING TARGET POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall maintain rankings for:
- videos
- streams
- broadcasters

# ============================================================
# 6. RANKING PERIOD POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall maintain at least:
- daily rankings
- monthly rankings
- yearly rankings
- overall rankings

These period scopes shall remain distinct.

# ============================================================
# 7. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall preserve native audience reactions
and native rankings
as internal platform features.

Reaction events and ranking results
shall remain distinct meanings.

purpose:
Defines the policy overview for
creator-side platform behavior in StreamingOS.

summary:

for self-management,
upload,
studio drafts,
publish preparation,
and creator-visible operational states.




<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/014.creator-platform-policy/0801400_STREAMING_OS_CREATOR_PLATFORM_POLICY.md -->
<!-- ========================================================= -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS CREATOR PLATFORM POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: creator-platform-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines creator-side platform rules
inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. CREATOR SELF-MANAGEMENT POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide creator-facing self-management surfaces
for:
- owned videos
- owned streams
- owned archives
- owned clips
- creator-visible revenue summaries
- creator-visible ranking summaries
- creator settings

These are self-facing control surfaces,
not public-facing channel surfaces.

# ============================================================
# 3. UPLOAD / STUDIO POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide native upload and studio preparation
for:
- upload intake
- draft save
- metadata setting
- thumbnail assignment
- visibility setting
- schedule setting
- review visibility
- publish request execution

# ============================================================
# 4. EDITING BOUNDARY POLICY
# ============================================================

Video editing may be provided by a separate application.

StreamingOS is not required to own
the full standalone video-editing function.

StreamingOS must own upload and publish preparation.

# ============================================================
# 5. DRAFT PRESERVATION POLICY
# ============================================================

Studio drafts should remain preservable,
resumable,
and cancellable.

Draft existence must remain explicit.

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide creator-facing self-management
and upload/studio preparation as native platform functions,
while preserving a boundary between
publish preparation
and standalone editing applications.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/015.viewer-discovery-and-continuity-policy/0801500_STREAMING_OS_VIEWER_DISCOVERY_AND_CONTINUITY_POLICY.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS VIEWER DISCOVERY AND CONTINUITY POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: viewer-discovery-and-continuity-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines viewer-side discovery
and continuity rules inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. DISCOVERY POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide native viewer entry surfaces for:
- home
- discovery
- ranking-linked discovery
- category discovery
- live-now discovery
- search

These meanings must remain distinguishable.

# ============================================================
# 3. SEARCH POLICY
# ============================================================

Explicit search shall remain distinct from recommendation.

Search history may be preserved
subject to platform-side visibility and management policy.

# ============================================================
# 4. FOLLOW / SUBSCRIPTION POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide native follow or subscription behavior
for channels,
creators,
or broadcaster-equivalent viewer targets.

Follow continuity must remain explicit.

# ============================================================
# 5. HISTORY / PLAYLIST / QUEUE POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall preserve distinct viewer continuity meanings for:
- watch history
- saved playlists
- temporary watch queues

These must not be collapsed into one generic viewing memory.

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide native viewer-side discovery,
search,
follow,
history,
playlist,
and watch queue behavior
as explicit internal platform features.

<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/080.policy/016.moderation-and-appeal-policy/0801600_STREAMING_OS_MODERATION_AND_APPEAL_POLICY.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS MODERATION AND APPEAL POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: moderation-and-appeal-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines moderation,
report,
appeal,
re-review,
and restoration rules inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. COMMENT MODERATION POLICY
# ============================================================

Comment moderation must preserve explicit states such as:
- visible
- hidden
- removed
- restricted
- restored

Moderation should not default to silent disappearance.

# ============================================================
# 3. REPORT POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall support explicit report submission.

Report existence,
review status,
and resolution outcome
must remain explicit.

# ============================================================
# 4. APPEAL / RE-REVIEW POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall support explicit appeal
and re-review request handling
for relevant moderation or review outcomes.

Appeal existence and final outcome must remain visible.

# ============================================================
# 5. RESTORATION POLICY
# ============================================================

Restoration must remain explicit and review-backed.

Restored state must not erase prior restriction history.

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide explicit moderation,
report,
appeal,
re-review,
and restoration handling
as native platform policy behavior.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS NOTIFICATION AND EVENT POLICY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: notification-and-event-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines notification
and platform-visible event rules
inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. CATEGORY DISTINCTION POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall preserve distinct visible event categories for:
- creator-facing notifications
- viewer-facing notifications
- operator-facing review alerts
- operator-facing escalation alerts

These must not be collapsed into one undifferentiated notice stream.

# ============================================================
# 3. DELIVERY POLICY
# ============================================================

Notification delivery behavior may vary operationally,
but canonical notification or event existence
must remain explicit inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 4. REVIEW / ESCALATION POLICY
# ============================================================

Review alerts and escalation alerts
are not ordinary viewer notifications.

They must remain separately identifiable.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall provide explicit notification
and platform-visible event policy
for creator,
viewer,
and operator-visible events.

# ============================================================

# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: corporate-channel-and-affiliation-policy
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. POLICY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This policy defines company-side channel
and affiliation rules inside StreamingOS.

# ============================================================
# 2. OFFICIAL CHANNEL POLICY
# ============================================================

Official company channels
shall preserve company ownership meaning explicitly.

Official company streaming should not be visually or semantically
collapsed into individual participation.

# ============================================================
# 3. AFFILIATED STREAMER POLICY
# ============================================================

Affiliated streamer participation
may remain visible and meaningful,
but does not by itself replace company ownership.

Affiliation meaning and ownership meaning
must remain distinct.

# ============================================================
# 4. COMPANY OVERSIGHT POLICY
# ============================================================

StreamingOS may provide company-side visibility
for official channels,
affiliated streamers,
and company-owned stream follow-up.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall preserve explicit policy distinctions between:
- official company ownership
- affiliated streamer participation
- company oversight visibility

layer: 080.policy

purpose:
Define policy rules for shared retry and dead-letter governance in StreamingOS.

policy_rules:

- exhausted, quarantined, or restored failure states must remain explicit
- failure traceability must remain preserved
- retry and dead-letter truth must remain canonical

retry_principles:
- retries must be bounded
- retries must not mask terminal failures
- retry identity must remain deterministic
- restoration must remain explicit

dead_letter_conditions:
- irrecoverable semantic failure
- terminal authority-basis failure
- exhausted retry budget
- incompatible contract profile
- missing target beyond cutoff

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS SECURITY OVERVIEW
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
layer: security
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

purpose:
Defines the security overview for StreamingOS.

summary:
The security layer defines
how StreamingOS protects creator,
viewer,
moderation,
notification,
corporate oversight,
publication,
reaction,
and monetization structures.

cross_device_support:
- iPhone
- Android
- PC
- tablet

international_support:
- multilingual operation
- multi-currency handling
- nation-aware currency display

security_principle:
- security meaning must remain device-neutral
- multilingual and multi-currency support
  must not weaken security boundaries
- canonical money meaning and display currency meaning
  must remain separate in access-control and policy interpretation

canonical_statement:
StreamingOS security is fixed
as a multilingual,
multi-currency,
cross-device security layer
across iPhone,
Android,
PC,
and tablet environments.

for creator,

corporate oversight,
and monetization protection.

<!-- ========================================================= -->
<!-- SOURCE: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/01.civilization-system/06.streaming-os/090.security/010.principal-and-session-security/0901000_STREAMING_OS_PRINCIPAL_AND_SESSION_SECURITY.md -->

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS PRINCIPAL AND SESSION SECURITY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: principal-and-session-security
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. SECURITY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This security domain defines how StreamingOS
protects stream principal integrity
and session execution integrity.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- principal identity integrity
- Civilization ID binding integrity
- session ownership integrity
- lifecycle transition integrity
- performer assignment authority integrity
- session control action integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. PRINCIPAL INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should ensure:
- principal is a valid Civilization subject
- principal type is eligible
- principal state is active when required
- ownership mode matches allowed principal type
- company-owned session uses company context where required

# ============================================================
# 4. SESSION CONTROL INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

Critical session control actions should be protected, including:
- move to ready
- start live
- pause
- resume
- end session
- cancel session
- lock session
- suspend session

Unauthorized or inconsistent control should be rejected.

# ============================================================
# 5. STATE INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

Session lifecycle transitions must be validated.
Runtime should reject:
- invalid state jumps
- replayed state mutations when not allowed
- ownership-inconsistent state actions
- actions from restricted or suspended contexts

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect
stream principal integrity
and session-state integrity
so that stream ownership and session control
cannot be altered by invalid or unauthorized actions.

# 1. SECURITY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This security domain defines how StreamingOS
protects formal monetization behavior.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- tip event integrity
- beneficiary resolution integrity
- source currency integrity
- conversion integrity
- display currency integrity
- hold/refund/reversal integrity
- auto tipping restriction integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. TIP INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should ensure:
- tip origin type is valid
- primary beneficiary resolution is valid
- session monetization context permits tipping
- held/refunded/reversed states are preserved distinctly
- fraudulent or suspicious activity can be isolated

# ============================================================
# 4. AUTO TIPPING SAFETY RULE
# ============================================================

For ai_emotional_auto tipping,
StreamingOS should enforce:
- Ai(human)-only eligibility by default
- total/per-stream/per-day limit checks
- balance availability checks
- minor-equivalent restrictions
- company policy restrictions when applicable
- governance restrictions when applicable
- stop-flag evaluation

# ============================================================
# 5. CONVERSION INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should preserve:
- source nation currency
- beneficiary nation currency
- conversion rule or reference
- converted amount
- display amount

These meanings must not be silently overwritten.

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect
formal tip integrity,
auto tipping limit enforcement,
and source-to-beneficiary currency conversion integrity
so that monetization meaning remains auditable and safe.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS EXTERNAL PUBLISH SECURITY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: external-publish-security
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. SECURITY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This security domain defines how StreamingOS
protects downstream external publication behavior.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- asset publish eligibility integrity
- rights review gate integrity
- safety review gate integrity
- company approval integrity when relevant
- publish job integrity
- publish result integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. REVIEW GATE ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should prevent external publication when:
- rights review is unresolved
- safety review is unresolved
- company approval is required but unresolved
- governance block is active

# ============================================================
# 4. RESULT INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should preserve:
- external video id
- external result status
- remote warnings
- remote errors
- visibility result

These must remain explicitly recorded.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect external publish execution
through review-gate enforcement
and result-record integrity
without allowing external publication state
to replace canonical internal asset ownership.

# ============================================================
# STREAMING OS REACTION RANKING AND AD ABUSE SECURITY
# ============================================================

status: canonical-draft
system: streaming-os
domain: reaction-ranking-and-ad-abuse-security
owner: Boss
prepared_by: Zero

# ============================================================
# 1. SECURITY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This security domain defines how StreamingOS
protects native reactions,
ranking integrity,
and internal ad behavior
from abuse or invalid amplification.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- reaction event validity
- favorite-state integrity
- reaction summary integrity
- ranking batch integrity
- ranking result integrity
- ad candidate resolution integrity
- ad insertion rule integrity
- ad display event integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. REACTION ABUSE RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should resist:
- invalid repeated reaction amplification
- contradictory reaction-state corruption
- invalid target reaction insertion
- unauthorized reaction mutation

# ============================================================
# 4. RANKING ABUSE RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should protect:
- ranking generation batches
- ranking score inputs
- ranking period separation
- ranking result persistence

Ranking outputs should not silently overwrite
raw reaction history.

# ============================================================
# 5. AD ABUSE RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should resist:
- invalid ad over-insertion
- insertion rule bypass
- fake display-event creation
- governance-block bypass for ads

# ============================================================
# 6. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect
native reactions,
ranking outputs,
and internal ad behavior
from manipulation or abuse
while preserving canonical raw history
and explicit result layers.

# ============================================================
# 1. SECURITY PURPOSE
# ============================================================

This security domain defines how StreamingOS
protects creator-side platform control surfaces.

# ============================================================
# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- creator self-page visibility integrity
- studio draft integrity
- upload preparation integrity
- publish-request authority integrity
- creator settings integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. AUTHORITY RULE
# ============================================================

Creator-facing control actions should be protected, including:
- save draft
- update metadata
- assign thumbnail
- update publish settings
- submit publish request
- update creator settings

Unauthorized actions should be rejected.

# ============================================================
# 4. SELF VS PUBLIC RULE
# ============================================================

Security must preserve the distinction between:
- creator self-facing management views
- public-facing channel or viewer-visible views

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect creator-side self-management,
studio draft handling,
and publish preparation authority
as explicit security responsibilities.

protects viewer discovery and continuity structures.

# 2. PRIMARY SECURITY OBJECTS
# ============================================================

Security focus includes:
- search history integrity
- watch history integrity
- follow/subscription integrity
- playlist ownership integrity
- watch queue integrity
- discovery access integrity

# ============================================================
# 3. OWNERSHIP / ACCESS RULE
# ============================================================

Viewer-side continuity structures must protect:
- owner-only modification for playlists and queues
- owner-only history management
- valid follow/unfollow mutation paths
- access boundaries for self-only continuity data

# ============================================================
# 4. DISCOVERY RULE
# ============================================================

Discovery visibility should not imply
unrestricted mutation authority
over viewer continuity structures.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect viewer-side discovery,
history,
follow,
playlist,
and queue structures
as explicit security responsibilities.

Security focus includes:

- moderation decision integrity
- appeal submission integrity
- re-review integrity
- restoration history integrity
- actor authority integrity for moderation actions

# ============================================================
# 3. DECISION INTEGRITY RULE
# ============================================================

StreamingOS should ensure:
- moderation actions are authorized
- report states are not silently rewritten
- appeal decisions are explicitly recorded
- restoration results remain auditable

# ============================================================
# 4. HISTORY PRESERVATION RULE
# ============================================================

Restriction and restoration history
must not be erasable through ordinary moderation mutation paths.

# ============================================================
# 5. CANONICAL FIXED STATEMENT
# ============================================================

StreamingOS shall protect moderation,
appeal,
re-review,
and restoration records
as explicit security responsibilities
with preserved auditable history.

must not erase canonical notification or event existence.

StreamingOS shall protect notifications,
platform events,
review alerts,
and escalation alerts
as explicit security responsibilities.

- company oversight visibility integrity
- company-owned stream control integrity

# ============================================================

- affiliated individual participation or visibility

and affiliated streamer references
as explicit security responsibilities
without collapsing ownership into participation.

purpose:
Defines exact authorization and access-control mapping
for major StreamingOS platform surfaces.
